ops.body: P1 close-out review verdict (sol finder+refuter on c8ec271, both fail): the supersede diff doesn't condition on sweep completeness. ACCEPTED all 7 findings (4 major, 3 minor; executed repros on each major): (1) a zero-row not_run/failed sweep still ingests as the latest generation, falsely superseding every prior row and destroying the diff baseline; (2) a quota-stopped partial sweep supersedes the prior fuller sweep's unreached rows — the disclosure-honesty guarantee didn't extend to the diff; (3) 'ran' is decided from rows not completed calls (empty page + refusal reads not_run despite calls=1); (4) widened-window rows are filtered out of the disclosure and vanish silently; (5-minor) ingest's BEGIN IMMEDIATE has no rollback path mid-ingest (narrow: fresh Store per leg run); (6-minor) calls counter misses returned-500 requests; (7-minor) validator accepts count/ids incoherence and not_run+superseded_rows contradictions. REJECTED: none. Fix ruling (fable synthesis): supersede requires a COMPLETE generation — only a fully-searched sweep creates a new generation + diff; incomplete sweeps upsert rows into the current generation (additive, no baseline replacement, no disclosure); diff scope bounds to the window actually searched this run; ran-ness from completed calls; a post-response error counts its call; connection-context transaction; validator coherence (unique ids, len==min(count,50), no superseded_rows on not_run). Fix dispatches after the in-flight P2a implementers release trips.py; ships as its own commit.
